sections.paragraph: pl. בְּקָעֹות, f. a valley (as if a cleaving and separation of mountains), opp. to mountains, Deu 8:7 11:11 Psa 104:8 to hills, Isa 41:18. But more often a plain country, widely extended plain (LXX. πεδίον), e.g. that in which Babylon was situated, Gen 11:2 comp. Eze 3:23 37:1 , Eze 37:2 בִּקְעַת הַלְּבָנֹון “the valley of Lebanon,” used of the plain at the foot of Hermon and Antilibanus, at the rise of Jordan, Jos 11:17 12:7 and not the valley between Libanus and Antilibanus, Cœlesyria of Strabo, Ard el Bŭkĉ’ǎ ارض البقا ع (land of vallies) of the Arabs. Other regions are called from towns near them, as בִּקְעַת מְגִדֹּון 2Ch 35:22 ב׳ יְרֵחֹו Deu 34:3. (Syriac ܦܘܰܢܬܐܳ, Arabic بَقيعُ, بُقْعَةُ and بَقْعَةُ id.)
